perturbation.original_text: Shipper and Transporter may be referred to herein individually as a "Party" or collectively as the "Parties".
perturbation.changed_text: Shipper and Transporter may be referred to herein individually as a "Party" or collectively as the "Entities".
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses 'Parties' to refer to Shipper and Transporter collectively. By changing this to 'Entities', it introduces ambiguity as to whether 'Parties' and 'Entities' are interchangeable throughout the document, especially in sections that define the rights and obligations of 'Parties'. This will create in-text contradiction in other locations where 'Parties' is being used, with the inserted perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Lien/Auction. (a) Transporter shall have a lien on all Crude Petroleum delivered to Transporter to secure the payment of any and all transportation, or any other charges that are owed Transporter. Such lien shall survive delivery of Crude Petroleum to Shipper. Such lien shall extend to all Crude Petroleum in Transporter's possession beginning with Shipper's first receipt of transportation or other services from Transporter. The lien provided herein shall be in addition to any lien or security interest provided by statute or applicable law. Transporter may withhold delivery to Shipper of any of Shipper's Crude Petroleum in its possession and exercise any other rights and remedies granted under this Agreement or existing under applicable law until all such charges have been paid as provided above. (b) If Shipper fails to pay the undisputed portion of an invoice by the due date, in addition to any other remedies under this Agreement or under applicable law, Transporter shall have the right, either directly or through an agent, to sell at a private sale any and all Crude Petroleum of such Shipper in its custody at fair market value at the time of sale. The proceeds of any sale shall be applied to the following order: (i) to the reasonable expenses of holding, preparing for sale, selling, and to the extent allowed by law, reasonable attorney's fees and legal expenses incurred by Transporter; and (ii) to the satisfaction of the Shipper's indebtedness including interest herein provided from the date of payment is due. The balance of the proceeds of the sale remaining, if any, shall be paid to Shipper or, if there is a dispute or claim as to entitlement, held for whoever may be lawfully entitled thereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Parties' as Shipper and Transporter. The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Entities' when referring to them collectively. The contradicted text repeatedly refers to the rights and obligations of 'Shipper' and 'Transporter' as 'Parties'. This introduces a contradiction because if 'Parties' is no longer a valid term to collectively refer to Shipper and Transporter, the contradicted text's references to 'Parties' become ambiguous and its enforceability is uncertain, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the other provisions of this Agreement, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall remain in effect until terminated by either Party upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the other provisions of this Contract, the term of this Contract shall commence on the Effective Date and shall remain in effect until terminated by either signatory upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Agreement' is changed to 'Contract', and 'Party' is changed to 'Signatory'. This change introduces confusion over whether 'Agreement' and 'Contract' are interchangeable, and whether 'Party' and 'Signatory' have the same rights and obligations. It creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement because several clauses refer to specific rights and obligations of the 'Parties' under the 'Agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16.12 No Third Party Beneficiaries. This Agreement is for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Parties hereto. Except as expressly provided herein to the contrary, nothing herein is intended to benefit any other Person not a Party hereto, and no such Person shall have any legal or equitable right, remedy or claim under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Contract' and 'Party' to 'Signatory' introduces ambiguity. The contradicted text (16.12) specifies that the 'Agreement' is for the benefit of the 'Parties'. If 'Contract' and 'Agreement' are not explicitly defined as interchangeable, a signatory who is not considered a party may attempt to claim benefits under the contract, contradicting section 16.12, which limits benefits to parties only. Similarly, the term 'Party' has specific rights and obligations which may or may not be transferred to 'Signatory'. This ambiguity introduces a contradiction regarding who can claim benefits under section 16.12.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not constitute a partnership or joint venture between Transporter and Shipper. Transporter shall carry out its operations with respect to the Gathering System as an independent contractor and shall not (except as expressly set forth in this Agreement) be subject to the control of Shipper in doing so.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract shall not constitute a partnership or joint venture between Transporter and Shipper. Transporter shall carry out its operations with respect to the Gathering System as an independent service provider and shall not (except as expressly set forth in this Contract) be subject to the control of Shipper in doing so.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses 'Agreement' and 'independent contractor'. By changing these to 'Contract' and 'independent service provider', it introduces ambiguity as to whether 'Agreement' and 'Contract' are interchangeable throughout the document, especially in sections that define the rights and obligations of 'Parties'. This will create in-text contradiction in other locations where 'Agreement' and 'Independent contractor' is being used, with the inserted perturbation. The reference to 'expressly set forth in this Contract' is intended to apply to 'independent service provider'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 Shipper shall release, indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Transporter and its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, consultants, representatives, and invitees from and against all claims and losses arising out of or relating to (i) the operations of Shipper, (ii) any breach of this agreement by Shipper, except to the extent attributable to the negligence, willful misconduct or fault of Transporter, and (iii) operations or activities upstream or downstream of the Gathering System, except to the extent attributable to the negligence, willful misconduct or fault of Transporter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Contract' and 'independent contractor' with 'independent service provider'. The contradicted text (13.1) refers to 'this agreement'. If 'Agreement' and 'Contract' are not explicitly defined as interchangeable, the indemnity clause might not apply to the 'Contract' as defined in the changed text. Additionally, the contradicted text mentions 'Transporter' who was previously described as an 'independent contractor'. By changing this to 'independent service provider', the indemnification may no longer clearly apply to the transporter in its new capacity. This introduces ambiguity and a strong possibility for in-text contradiction, falling under Misaligned Terminology, as the terms are used interchangeably without definition, potentially conflicting obligations.
